title: Strait of Hormuz chokepoint atlas — IEA + EIA primary-source facts
purpose: Single source of truth for every quantitative claim made by the SupplyMind War Room about Hormuz throughput, bypass capacity, and global oil/LNG transit. Every value is cited to a public URL; no model-generated numbers in this file.
curation_policy: Each fact has source_type ∈ {primary, secondary, model_estimate}. We do not include model_estimate in this file; only primary/secondary. The endpoint may produce model_estimate values at runtime but they go to a separate response field with explicit labelling.

facts.claim: Approximately 20 million barrels per day of crude oil and refined products moved through the Strait of Hormuz in 2024.
facts.title: World Oil Transit Chokepoints — Hormuz
facts.retrieved_note: EIA designates Hormuz as the world's most important oil chokepoint.

facts.claim: Hormuz transit represents roughly 25% of global seaborne oil trade.
facts.title: Oil Market Report — Strait of Hormuz commentary
facts.retrieved_note: IEA OMR commentary, multiple 2024 issues. Same number appears in IEA Oil 2024 (annual report).

facts.claim: Approximately 80% of crude and condensate transiting Hormuz is destined for Asian markets (China, India, Japan, Korea, Singapore).
facts.title: Today in Energy — Strait of Hormuz
facts.retrieved_note: EIA tracking shows ~82% of crude+condensate from Hormuz went to Asia in 2022; ~80% used as round figure.

facts.claim: Approximately 20% of global liquefied natural gas (LNG) trade transited the Strait of Hormuz in 2024.
facts.title: Today in Energy — LNG transit through Hormuz
facts.retrieved_note: EIA: Qatar (~77 MTPA) and UAE (~5 MTPA) LNG exports flow through Hormuz; combined ~20% of 2024 global LNG trade of ~415 MTPA.

facts.claim: Existing pipelines that bypass Hormuz (Saudi Arabia East–West / Petroline; UAE Habshan–Fujairah / ADCOP) have an effective spare bypass capacity of approximately 3.5–5.5 mb/d as of 2024.
facts.title: Oil Market Report and IEA chokepoint commentary
facts.retrieved_note: Petroline ~5 mb/d nameplate, ADCOP ~1.5 mb/d nameplate; effective spare after current utilization is the 3.5–5.5 mb/d band IEA cites. Far below full Hormuz throughput of ~20 mb/d.

facts.claim: Qatar's entire LNG export capacity (≈77 MTPA) ships exclusively through Hormuz; no overland or alternative seaborne route exists at scale.
facts.title: QatarEnergy LNG production capacity disclosures
facts.retrieved_note: Confirmed by IGU 2024 World LNG Report (https://www.igu.org/resources/2024-world-lng-report).

facts.claim: UAE crude exports via Fujairah (which lies outside Hormuz) account for approximately 1.5 mb/d, providing partial but not full UAE bypass.
facts.title: UAE oil flows through Fujairah — Reuters market reporting
facts.retrieved_note: ADCOP pipeline + direct UAE production; figure consistent across multiple Reuters/S&P 2024 dispatches.
